sentence: She looked up the word in the dictionary
explanation: <Look up> means to search for information in a reference.

sentence: He came across an old photo in the drawer
explanation: <Come across> means to find something by chance.

sentence: They put off the meeting until next Monday
explanation: <Put off> means to postpone or delay.

sentence: We need to figure out a solution to this problem
hint: Subject + need to + phrasal verb + object
explanation: <Figure out> means to find the answer or understand something.

sentence: She turned down the invitation to the party
explanation: <Turn down> means to refuse or reject.

sentence: He ran into his old teacher at the supermarket
explanation: <Run into> means to meet someone unexpectedly.

sentence: They called off the football match because of the rain
She takes after her mother in many ways
explanation: <Take after> means to resemble a family member.

sentence: We should look into this matter more carefully
hint: Subject + modal + phrasal verb + object + adverb
explanation: <Look into> means to investigate or examine.

sentence: He brought up an interesting point during the discussion
explanation: <Bring up> means to mention or introduce a topic.

sentence: She got over her cold after a few days of rest
explanation: <Get over> means to recover from an illness or difficulty.

sentence: They set up a new business in the city centre
explanation: <Set up> means to establish or start something new.
